words.def: a long piece of writing completed by a student as part of a university degree, based on their own research
words.example: Students must submit a thesis on an agreed subject within four years.

words.def: done completely; with great attention to detail
words.example: a thorough knowledge of the subject

words.def: very much; completely
words.example: We thoroughly enjoyed ourselves.

words.def: used especially at the end of a sentence or clause to add a fact or an opinion that makes the previous statement less strong or less important
words.example: Our team lost. It was a good game though.

words.def: a statement in which you tell somebody that you will punish or harm them, especially if they do not do what you want
words.example: threat against somebody/something, to make threats against somebody

words.def: to say that you will cause trouble, hurt somebody, etc. if you do not get what you want
words.example: threaten somebody/something, They broke my windows and threatened me.

words.def: a passage in the neck through which food and air pass on their way into the body; the front part of the neck
words.example: a sore throat

words.def: the short, thick finger at the side of the hand, slightly apart from the other four
words.example: She still sucks her thumb when she's worried.

words.def: in this way; like this
words.example: Many scholars have argued thus.

words.def: what is measured in minutes, hours, days, etc.
words.example: The world exists in time and space.

words.def: the act of choosing when something happens; a particular point or period of time when something happens or is planned
words.example: The timing of the decision was a complete surprise.

words.def: a piece of soft paper, used especially as a handkerchief
words.example: a box of tissues

words.def: the name of a book, poem, painting, piece of music, etc.
words.example: What's title of her new book?

words.def: a unit for measuring weight, in the UK 2 240 pounds (long ton) and in the US 2 000 pounds (short ton)
words.example: (informal), What have you got in this bag? It weighs a ton (= is very heavy).

words.def: the quality of somebody’s voice, especially expressing a particular emotion
words.example: speaking in hushed/low/clipped/measured, etc. tones

words.def: a unit for measuring weight, equal to 1 000 kilograms
words.example: a record grain harvest of 236m tonnes

words.def: having or causing problems or difficulties
words.example: a tough childhood

words.def: a sports competition involving a number of teams or players who take part in different games and must leave the competition if they lose. The competition continues until there is only the winner left.
words.example: a golf/tennis/soccer/chess tournament

words.def: rails (= metal bars) that a train moves along
words.example: railway/railroad tracks

words.def: the activity of buying and selling things
words.example: new laws on Sunday trading (= shops being open on Sundays)

words.def: a very sad event or situation, especially one that involves death
words.example: It's a tragedy that she died so young.

words.def: making you feel very sad, usually because somebody has died or suffered a lot
words.example: He was killed in a tragic accident at the age of 24.

words.def: a shoe that you wear for sports or as informal clothing
words.example: a pair of trainers

words.def: a particular quality in your personality
words.example: personality traits

words.def: the act of moving somebody/something from one place, group or job to another; an occasion when this happens
words.example: electronic data transfer

words.def: to change the form of something; to change in form
words.example: transform something/somebody (into something), The photochemical reactions transform the light into electrical impulses.

words.def: the process or a period of changing from one state or condition to another
words.example: transition from something to something, the transition from school to full-time work

words.def: the process of changing something that is written or spoken into another language
words.example: an error in translation

words.def: to send an electronic signal, radio or television broadcast, etc.
words.example: signals transmitted from a satellite

words.def: a system for carrying people or goods from one place to another using vehicles, roads, etc.
words.example: public transportation (= the system of buses, trains, etc. provided for people to travel from one place to another)

words.def: a piece of equipment for catching animals
words.example: a fox with its leg in a trap

words.def: a collection of valuable things such as gold, silver and jewellery
words.example: buried treasure

words.def: a formal examination of evidence in court by a judge and often a jury, to decide if somebody accused of a crime is guilty or not
words.example: a murder/criminal trial

words.def: a social group in a traditional society consisting of people with the same language, culture, religion, etc., living in a particular area and often having one leader known as a chief
words.example: They were a nomadic horse-riding tribe.

words.def: a journey to a place and back again, especially a short one for pleasure or a particular purpose
words.example: Did you have a good trip?

words.def: soldiers, especially in large groups
words.example: They announced the withdrawal of 12 000 troops from the area.

words.def: coming from, found in or typical of the tropics
words.example: tropical fish/birds/fruit

words.def: a problem, worry, difficulty, etc. or a situation causing this
words.example: We have trouble getting staff.

words.def: used to emphasize that a particular statement, feeling, etc. is sincere or real
words.example: She truly believes that none of this is her fault.

words.def: the belief that somebody/something is good, sincere, honest, etc. and will not try to harm or trick you
words.example: Our partnership is based on trust.

words.def: an act of trying to do something
words.example: I doubt they'll be able to help but it's worth a try (= worth asking them).

words.def: an extremely large wave in the sea caused, for example, by an earthquake
words.example: A tsunami early warning system was set up in Hawaii.

words.def: a series of musical notes that are sung or played in a particular order to form a piece of music
words.example: to sing/whistle a tune

words.def: a passage built underground, for example to allow a road or railway to go through a hill, under a river, etc.
words.example: a railway/railroad tunnel

words.def: happening at the end of a long process
words.example: our ultimate goal/aim/objective/target

words.def: in the end; finally
words.example: A poor diet will ultimately lead to illness.

words.def: so bad that you think it should not be allowed
words.example: Such behaviour is totally unacceptable in a civilized society.

words.def: the state of being uncertain
words.example: There is considerable uncertainty about the company's future.
